alternative_parents.description: Inorganic salts containing a lithium ion.
alternative_parents.name: Inorganic lithium salts

alternative_parents.description: Inorganic compounds in which hydrogen is bonded to a more electropositive element or group. Usually, they contain one or more hydrogen centers having nucleophilic, reducing, or basic properties.
alternative_parents.name: Inorganic hydrides

class.description: Inorganic compounds belonging either to the alkali metal hydrides, the alkali metal nitrides, the alkali metal oxides, or the alkali metal sulfides.
class.name: Alkali metal organides

description: This compound belongs to the class of inorganic compounds known as alkali metal hydrides. These are inorganic hydride compounds in which the heaviest metal atom is an alkali metal.

direct_parent.description: Inorganic hydride compounds in which the heaviest metal atom is an alkali metal.
direct_parent.name: Alkali metal hydrides

kingdom.description: Compounds that do not contain a C atom. Exceptions are made for isocyanide/cyanide and their non-hydrocarbyl derivatives, thiophosgene, carbon diselenide, carbon monosulfide, carbon disulfide, carbon subsulfide, carbon monoxide, carbon trioxide, carbon suboxide, and dicarbon monoxide.
kingdom.name: Inorganic compounds

subclass.description: Inorganic hydride compounds in which the heaviest metal atom is an alkali metal.
subclass.name: Alkali metal hydrides

superclass.description: Compounds that contains both metal and non metal atoms.
superclass.name: Mixed metal/non-metal compounds
